data.content:
Implementation plan:
1. Trace the write path in src/write_dispatch.rs and centralize lease-derived metadata resolution for update operations so both note and handoff creation use the bound lease once, not repeated ad hoc fallbacks.
2. Map lease identity fields from src/domain/lease.rs into metadata fields consistently: provider -> username, agent_name -> agentname, model -> model, model_version -> version. Preserve explicit --note-* and --handoff-* flags as higher priority, and keep "unknown" only for fields still empty after both explicit input and lease metadata are checked.
3. Keep persistence in the normal write path so src/app.rs continues storing the resolved metadata directly in knot.note_added and knot.handoff_capsule_added events. This should satisfy show/read behavior without adding repair logic on read, because kno show --json already projects persisted metadata entries.
4. Extend lease-focused tests in src/write_dispatch/tests_lease_ext.rs to cover both add-note and add-handoff-capsule with a bound lease and no identity flags, asserting username/agentname/model/version are stamped from lease identity and not stored as "unknown". Add override coverage to prove explicit flags still win, and keep the no-lease fallback case so legacy unknown behavior remains only when no lease metadata exists.
5. Add an end-to-end acceptance test at the CLI or app layer (tests/cli_dispatch.rs or src/app/tests_acceptance_ext.rs) that binds a lease, runs update operations without identity flags, then verifies kno show <id> --json returns the already-persisted stamped metadata for both notes and handoff capsules.
Complexity: small to moderate, likely confined to write_dispatch plus targeted tests.
Primary risk: confirming the intended source for metadata.username. Based on the current schema, lease agent_info.provider is the only durable lease identity field that can populate username, so tests should lock that mapping in explicitly.
